title: Matterbridge Roomba Plugin
description: iRobot Roomba vacuum cleaners as standalone Matter devices

testCloudLogin.title: Test cloud login
testCloudLogin.description: Toggle on and Confirm to run a one-off iRobot cloud login test. Results appear in the Matterbridge logs. Toggle is auto-reset.

applyDiscoveredRooms.title: Save discovered rooms to config
applyDiscoveredRooms.description: Toggle on and Confirm after running discovery mode (clean each room once from the iRobot app). Snapshots the captured regions into each device's rooms/pmapId/userPmapvId. Rename rooms afterwards in the Devices section. Toggle is auto-reset.

cloud.description: Optional iRobot account credentials. When set, the plugin auto-fetches each robot's BLID and local MQTT password so you don't need to extract them manually. Only used at startup — stored credentials are not revalidated thereafter.

countryCode.description: Two-letter ISO country code for the iRobot cloud discovery endpoint (e.g. US, DE, JP). Defaults to US; Gigya federates so it usually doesn't matter.

devices.description: List of Roomba devices to connect to

blid.description: Robot BLID (unique identifier). Auto-filled when the top-level cloud.email/password are set. Otherwise find it via 'get-roomba-password' or the iRobot app.

password.description: Robot's LOCAL MQTT password (not your iRobot account password). Auto-filled when cloud credentials are set.

ipAddress.description: Robot IP address on your local network. Must be supplied manually — cloud lookup does not return it.

refreshInterval.description: State polling interval in seconds (while active)

idleRefreshInterval.description: State polling interval in seconds (while idle/docked)

serverMode.description: Expose this robot as its own independent Matter device (separate QR code and pairing) instead of a bridged endpoint. Recommended for Apple Home and Google Home — they handle standalone vacuums better than bridged ones. Turn off only if you prefer a single pairing code for all Matterbridge plugins.

vendor.description: Vendor name shown in Matter controllers (default: iRobot)

model.description: Model name shown in Matter controllers (default: robot-reported SKU, e.g. 'j517020')

discoverRooms.description: When true, the plugin logs a copy-paste-ready config block whenever a room-based clean command is observed. See ONBOARDING.md for the step-by-step flow. Turn off after you have filled in rooms/pmapId/userPmapvId.

roomCleanSqft.description: Estimated square feet Roomba cleans per room during a multi-room mission. Used to advance which room shows as 'currently cleaning' in your Matter controller as the robot works through selected rooms. This is the primary signal because Roomba reports cumulative cleaned sqft and it pauses naturally during recharge/stuck events. Default 75.

roomCleanDurationMinutes.description: Fallback estimated minutes per room, used only when Roomba doesn't emit sqft (rare on modern firmware). Default 10.

verboseState.description: Dev-only: log raw MQTT state deltas (cleanMissionStatus, lastCommand, pose, bbmssn). Enable if troubleshooting per-room progress or other state-related issues.

iosAllRoomsWorkaround.description: Work around an iOS Home UI bug: picking 'All Rooms' leaves the picker checkboxes showing only the first room until the subscription update lands. When ON (default), the plugin mirrors the full room list back to the SelectedAreas attribute so iOS Home's display matches intent within a few seconds. NOTE: this technically violates Matter spec §1.17.6.4 (SelectedAreas should only be updated via the SelectAreas command). Turn OFF once Apple fixes the iOS Home bug, or if you're on macOS Home only (which handles the spec-compliant empty-list semantic correctly without help).

pmapId.description: Persistent map identifier (auto-discovered). Required for room-targeted cleans on single-map setups. For multi-floor homes use the maps array instead.

userPmapvId.description: User map version identifier (auto-discovered). Required for room-targeted cleans.

maps.description: Multi-floor setups: one entry per Roomba persistent map. Each room's mapId picks which map it's on. Skip this if you only have one map — the top-level pmapId/userPmapvId will apply to every room.

mapId.description: Unique Matter map identifier (uint32) used to reference this map from each room's mapId field. Pick something stable like 1, 2, 3.

name.description: Human-readable label shown in the Matter controller (e.g. 'Main Floor', 'Upstairs').

pmapId.description: Roomba's persistent map identifier for this floor (capture in discovery mode while the robot is on this floor).

userPmapvId.description: User map version identifier that pairs with this pmapId.

rooms.description: Rooms/areas to expose as Matter service areas. Populated automatically by discovery mode. Leave empty to hide the rooms selector.

areaId.description: Stable Matter area identifier presented to controllers (uint32). Generated from the robot's regionId by discovery mode.

regionId.description: Robot-internal region identifier (auto-discovered). Used to translate Matter selectAreas requests back to dorita980 cleanRoom calls.

regionType.description: Region type reported by the robot (rid=room, zid=zone). Auto-discovered; defaults to rid.

name.description: Display name for the room shown in your Matter controller

type.description: Semantic room type — maps to a Matter AreaNamespaceTag so controllers can pick an icon. Examples: LivingRoom, Kitchen, Bedroom, Bathroom, Office, Hallway, Dining, Laundry, Garage, PrimaryBedroom, GuestBedroom

mapId.description: If `maps` is configured (multi-floor home), this points the room at one of those maps. Falls back to the first configured map if omitted. Ignored when `maps` is empty.
